data.description:

The latest announcement is out from Brambles ( (AU:BXB) ).

Brambles has disclosed a change in the indirect security holdings of director Graham Andrew Chipchase, as required under Australian Securities Exchange listing rules. The notice records an indirect interest in 92 additional ordinary shares issued under the Brambles Limited MyShare Plan Distribution Reinvestment Plan, held on his behalf by Certane SPV Management Pty Ltd.

The updated disclosure reflects routine adjustments to a director’s equity position rather than a strategic transaction by the company, but it adds incremental transparency for investors tracking insider interests. By keeping the market informed of such movements, Brambles adheres to governance and compliance requirements that support shareholder confidence in its oversight framework.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:BXB) stock is a Hold with a A$24.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Brambles stock, see the AU:BXB Stock Forecast page.

More about Brambles

Brambles Limited is a supply-chain logistics company headquartered in Sydney, Australia, best known for its global pallet and container pooling services that support fast‑moving consumer goods, retail, and industrial customers. The group operates primarily through its CHEP and IFCO brands, providing shared and reusable logistics platforms across key international markets.

data.title: Rome Resources Reports Strongest Tin Intercepts Yet at DRC’s Kalayi Prospect
data.description:
Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Pathfinder Minerals ( (GB:RMR) ) has issued an update.

Rome Resources has completed its latest drilling campaign at the Kalayi prospect within the Bisie North project in the DRC, finishing 17 holes for 3,050 metres, 91% of which targeted Kalayi. Management reports that portable XRF readings indicate high-grade tin mineralisation continuing at depth and across multiple holes, and that the current campaign has delivered the widest and richest intercepts recorded at the prospect so far.

The company expects formal laboratory assays in the coming weeks, which will feed into an updated Mineral Resource Estimate for Kalayi. If confirmed, the stronger intercepts and uplifted resource base could enhance the project’s economic case and Rome Resources’ strategic position in a tin district that already hosts a mine producing a significant share of global supply.

The most recent analyst rating on (GB:RMR) stock is a Hold with a £0.24 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Pathfinder Minerals stock, see the GB:RMR Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on RMR Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, RMR is a Neutral.

The score is primarily pressured by weak financial performance (no revenue, widening losses, and persistent negative free cash flow implying continued funding risk). Technicals add a modest headwind due to a weak longer-term trend and negative MACD, while valuation is constrained because negative earnings make P/E less informative and there is no dividend support.

To see Spark’s full report on RMR stock, click here.

More about Pathfinder Minerals

Rome Resources is an exploration company focused on tin and copper projects in the Democratic Republic of Congo. Its flagship Bisie North project lies around 8 km from Alphamin’s Mpama mine, positioning the group in a globally significant tin-producing district with potential exposure to high-grade, near-surface mineralisation.

data.title: Gemfields updates G-Factor metrics, highlighting shifting government take at Zambia and Mozambique mines
data.description:
Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Gemfields Group ( (PLLHF) ) has issued an update.

Gemfields has updated its “G-Factor for Natural Resources” metrics to the end of 2025 for its key operations, showing long-term government-take ratios of 17% at Zambia’s Kagem emerald mine and 26% at Mozambique’s Montepuez Ruby Mining. The G-Factor expresses the share of a mine’s revenue paid to host governments via royalties, corporation tax, dividends and export levies, and is intended as a transparency tool for natural resource revenues.

At Montepuez, the one-year G-Factor for 2025 was 23%, with US$11.3 million paid to the Mozambican government on revenue of US$49.9 million, a strong proportional contribution despite lower cash payments due to weaker premium-ruby output, a delayed auction and illegal mining disruptions. At Kagem, the 2025 G-Factor fell sharply to 6%, reflecting a four-month shutdown and a temporary 15% export tax that compounded poor market conditions, although Gemfields expects the mine’s fiscal contribution to revert toward its roughly 18% long-term average as operations normalise.

By continuing to publish the G-Factor annually for its subsidiaries, Gemfields is positioning itself as a proponent of fiscal transparency in the mining sector and urging broader adoption across extractive industries. The metric offers host governments and citizens a simple indicator of how efficiently their natural resources are converted into public revenues, even while the company acknowledges its limitations and the exclusion of various indirect taxes from the calculation.

More about Gemfields Group

Gemfields Group is a Guernsey-incorporated, dual-listed miner of coloured gemstones with shares on the Johannesburg Stock Exchange and London’s AIM market. The company operates and owns 75% of Kagem Mining in Zambia, one of the world’s leading emerald mines, and Montepuez Ruby Mining in Mozambique, a major recent ruby discovery, and also holds other gemstone licences in Africa.

Gemfields has built a proprietary grading system and auction platform to deliver a consistent and transparent supply of coloured gemstones to downstream markets. This model has supported the expansion of the global coloured gemstone sector and underpins the firm’s strategic focus on responsible resource stewardship and value sharing with host countries.

Brown Advisory US Smaller Companies PLC has undertaken a share buyback, purchasing 10,000 of its ordinary shares at 1,382.36 pence each to be held in treasury, a move that can help enhance net asset value per share and manage the discount to the share price. Following this transaction, the company’s issued share capital stands at 18,223,413 ordinary shares, of which 6,933,543 are held in treasury, leaving 11,289,870 shares in circulation as the relevant figure for regulatory disclosure purposes under FCA rules.

The updated share count means investors and other stakeholders should now use the 11,289,870 figure when assessing percentage holdings and determining whether they must notify the market of interests or changes in interests in the company. data.title: Mercantile Ports & Logistics Steps Up Legal Fight to Recover Karanja Asset and Redeem Debt in Full
data.description:
Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Mercantile Ports and Logistics ( (GB:MPL) ) has provided an update.

Mercantile Ports & Logistics is intensifying legal efforts in India to regain control of its wholly owned Karanja Terminal & Logistics asset, after a previously agreed one-time debt settlement was annulled and the asset was pushed into an insolvency process. The company has offered to redeem 100% of its outstanding debt, but says the Committee of Creditors led by Prudent ARC has yet to properly consider its proposal despite court directions.

MPL has secured backing from a major international oil and gas company and other investors to fully refinance and repay financial creditors while funding future development of the Karanja project. Management argues the process appears skewed toward third-party resolution plans that could shift the asset away from MPL and its international shareholders, raising concerns over process integrity and the treatment of foreign capital in India’s insolvency framework.

The most recent analyst rating on (GB:MPL) stock is a Hold with a £0.41 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Mercantile Ports and Logistics stock, see the GB:MPL Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on MPL Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, MPL is a Underperform.

Overall score is held back primarily by weak financial performance (ongoing losses, negative operating margins, strained free cash flow and rising leverage). Technicals are also unfavorable with the price below key longer-term moving averages and a negative MACD, while valuation is difficult to support due to a negative P/E and no provided dividend yield.

To see Spark’s full report on MPL stock, click here.

More about Mercantile Ports and Logistics

Mercantile Ports & Logistics operates and develops a port and logistics facility in Navi Mumbai, Maharashtra, India, centred on its Karanja Terminal & Logistics asset. The company has invested about £160 million of mainly international equity capital over 15 years, building infrastructure despite regulatory delays and infrastructure challenges, and the facility remains revenue generating.

Average Trading Volume: 479,188

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: £1.6M

Hafnia ( (HAFN) ) has issued an update.

Hafnia’s Chief Financial Officer, Perry Van Echtelt, has sold 90,000 Hafnia shares on the Oslo Børs at an average price of NOK 79.5052 per share, with the company stating the primary purpose of the transaction is to cover incurred tax liabilities. The sale, disclosed under EU Market Abuse Regulation and Norwegian securities rules, represents a routine personal financial move by a senior executive rather than a strategic change for Hafnia’s operations, but will nonetheless be closely watched by investors given his key role in the company’s financial stewardship.

The company noted that the transaction has been reported as a mandatory notification of trade, underscoring Hafnia’s adherence to transparency and regulatory disclosure standards for dealings by primary insiders. While the sale does not alter Hafnia’s tanker-focused business model or fleet operations, it highlights ongoing scrutiny of insider transactions in the shipping sector and may prompt stakeholders to monitor future trades by top management for any potential signals about management’s outlook or capital needs.

More about Hafnia

Hafnia Limited is one of the world’s leading tanker owners, transporting crude oil, refined oil products, and chemicals for major national and international oil and chemical companies, as well as trading and utility firms. Operating a fleet of around 200 vessels with integrated technical, commercial, and chartering services, the BW Group-backed company runs global offices in Singapore, Copenhagen, Houston, and Dubai, employing more than 4,000 staff at sea and onshore.

MA Credit Income Trust ( (AU:MA1) ) has provided an announcement.

MA Credit Income Trust has updated its notification to the ASX regarding a monthly distribution on its fully paid ordinary units, relating to the period ended 31 March 2026. The revised announcement, dated 9 April 2026 and amending details first released on 23 March 2026, reflects changes to the actual distribution amount and the Distribution Reinvestment Plan price, clarifying key dates such as the ex date of 31 March and record date of 1 April for unitholders.

The update ensures investors have accurate parameters for the March distribution and any participation in the DRP, providing greater transparency around income timing and reinvestment terms.
